claim: Break monolithic LLM prompts into staged, multi-step agentic workflows with explicit specialist stages to improve debuggability and identify where failures occur
actionable: Decompose your single-shot LLM call into 5+ sequential stages (e.g., context collection, triage, policy review, reply generation, escalation decision) where each stage has its own prompt and can be traced and evaluated independently.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Integrate local tool calls into agentic workflows to make outputs more deterministic and grounded in real data
actionable: Add tool calling layers that fetch help desk articles, customer account history, policy checks, and other deterministic data before the LLM makes decisions, reducing hallucinations and improving accuracy.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Instrument AI applications with nested span tracing to capture the full execution path including latency, token counts, costs, and metadata for observability
actionable: Wrap every LLM call and function invocation in parent/child spans using an observability SDK (e.g., Braintrust SDK), capturing inputs, outputs, latency, token usage, and custom metadata to enable debugging production failures.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Create golden test datasets before deploying to production to establish baseline confidence in agentic systems
actionable: Design 10-20 edge-case test scenarios representing your application's failure modes, tag them with expected outcomes, and run them through your agent to establish a golden dataset for offline evaluation before shipping.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Use deterministic scoring functions for cheap, always-on evaluations and LLM-as-judge scoring for subjective quality assessment that requires nuance
actionable: Implement two scoring layers: deterministic checks (schema validation, policy compliance) that run on 100% of cases, and LLM-as-judge rubrics (tone, helpfulness, reasoning correctness) that run on a 5-10% sampling rate after establishing baseline.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Manage prompts and parameters in a centralized system rather than versioning them in code to enable non-technical stakeholders to iterate without engineering handoffs
actionable: Move prompts, tools, and model parameters into a managed platform (e.g., Braintrust) with version history and UI editing so product managers and subject-matter experts can safely test prompt changes and compare model performance without touching code.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Run online scoring on production traces in real-time by applying deterministic and LLM-as-judge scoring functions to incoming logs as they arrive
actionable: Set up automation rules that apply evaluation scoring functions to live production traces: run deterministic scorers at 100% sampling, LLM-as-judge scorers at 5-10% sampling to manage costs while continuously monitoring quality.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Identify production failures via tracing, isolate them in a test dataset, modify the prompt, and re-evaluate against the full golden dataset to validate fixes before re-deploying
actionable: When online monitoring detects a production failure, pull the trace, add it to a remediation dataset, modify the problematic prompt, run the evaluation suite against both the remediation case and full golden set to ensure no regressions, then redeploy.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Traditional software engineering rigor (unit tests, version control, change tracking, reproducibility) applies to agentic systems but must account for non-determinism in LLM outputs
actionable: Apply deterministic testing practices (schema checks, tool call validation) on 100% of cases like unit tests, then layer probabilistic evaluation (LLM-as-judge, user satisfaction metrics) on representative samples to balance rigor with LLM non-determinism.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Model switching (e.g., from GPT-4 to GPT-4-mini, Claude 3 to Sonnet) requires offline and online evaluation to ensure performance doesn't degrade before deploying
actionable: Before switching models in production, run your golden dataset through both models offline and compare scores side-by-side, then run online evaluation on real traffic at the new model for 24-48 hours before full rollout.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Observability (tracing and monitoring) is table stakes for production agentic systems; without it, you cannot debug failures or understand root causes
actionable: If you have a production AI application without tracing, stop and instrument it immediately with an observability solution that captures nested spans, latency, token counts, and custom metadata.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Production failures in agentic systems often stem not from model quality but from operational rigor gaps—lack of tracing, evaluation, version control, and failure-mode remediation workflows
actionable: Diagnose failing agentic systems by first establishing: (1) comprehensive tracing, (2) golden dataset + evaluation scores, (3) version control of prompts/parameters, (4) automated remediation workflow. Usually the problem is operational rigor, not model intelligence.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Use a continuous flywheel: collect data → identify failure modes → remediate → ship → monitor → repeat, starting with golden datasets and graduating to production logs
actionable: Set up the evaluation flywheel: start with hand-crafted golden test cases, evaluate your agent, identify failures, modify prompts/tools, re-evaluate, deploy to production, monitor with online scoring, pull failing cases back into the dataset, and loop continuously.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline

claim: Scale agentic systems by moving tools, prompts, and evaluation from local execution to a managed, secure platform that supports automation and cross-functional collaboration
actionable: After validating locally, deploy your prompts, tools, and scoring functions to a managed platform (via API/CLI) so that non-engineers can modify parameters, all changes are versioned and auditable, and evaluations run at scale.
source_title: Shipping complex AI applications — Braintrust & Trainline
